_comment: Generic starter profile — the canonical move-class vocabulary, with NEUTRAL weights and NO real user's pick-history. A new user begins here; the app grows their personal weights from their own picks. The menu must span >=5 distinct classes per turn.
menu_rules: Rank by LIVE CONTEXT first (current repo state, last action, open file, the message just sent), then by this profile's move-class weights, then standing moves in the tail. Never suggest into a blank canvas: with no context yet, item 1 is an orienting question, not a guessed plan. Mark outward/irreversible items (send/deploy/push/delete/post) with a leading warn flag. When the next several moves are high-confidence, low-risk, and same-thread, offer one 'story loop N' item.

signature_moves.desc: accept the result and take the obvious next step
accept, then widen the scope or chase a bigger version
run the proposed action but change one axis (scope, target, model)
turn the working thing into a test, hook, doc, or rule
commit / open a PR / publish (outward — mark warn)
question a claim, risk, or weak spot before continuing
save a decision, preference, or finding for later
switch to a different thread, file, or open task
produce a draft to read before it goes anywhere
step up a level — implications, positioning, what this signals
